---
title: "Song Transcriptions"
subtitle: "Trad. — with arrangement and annotation notes"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: songs.css
    toc: true
    toc-title: "Contents"
    embed-resources: true
---

<!-- ================================================================
SCHEMA REFERENCE — span classes (inline):
  [text]{.chord}        chord symbol
  [text]{.strike}       struck-through word/phrase
  [text]{.ins}          inserted replacement
  [text]{.hl-green}     yellow-green highlight
  [text]{.hl-pink}      pink highlight
  [text]{.hl-blue}      blue highlight
  [text]{.hl-orange}    orange highlight
  [text]{.hl-yellow}    yellow highlight
  [text]{.circled}      circled reference
  [text]{.side-cue}     right-margin performance cue
  [text]{.stage}        spoken aside / stage direction
  [text]{.blue-ink}     handwritten note in blue ink
  [text]{.cut}          line marked ✕ for possible removal
  [text]{.arrow}        arrow annotation

Block divs:
  ::: {.verse}          verse or section block
  ::: {.bracket-group}  lines grouped with a left bracket
  ::: {.annotation}     handwritten marginal note
  ::: {.alt}            alternative version of a line
  ::: {.structure-label} section header (Intro, Outro, BUT…)
  ::: {.side-box}       boxed aside panel
  ::: {.corner-note}    free-floating marginal note
  ::: {.song}           top-level container for one song
================================================================ -->


## Song 1 — (Reluctant Fisherman) {.song}

*Trad.*

::: {.structure-label}
\[A\]
:::

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
Oh me dad was a fisherman bold

And he lived til he grew old. [For…]{.side-cue}

::: {.annotation .blue-ink}
For he'd open the panes & pop out the flame / Just to see how the wind do blow
:::

And he'd often say to me

You'd be wise before you go. [Do…]{.side-cue}
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
When the North wind rough do blow

Then I lay right smug below. [But…]{.side-cue}
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
When the wind comes out of the East

It's no use to man nor beast. [But…]{.side-cue}

::: {.alt}
East – wind out of the East\
[Poor]{.strike} [Yosh]{.ins} wife – We'll regret if
:::
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
When the wind's back in to the west

That'll come in rough at best. [But…]{.side-cue}

::: {.alt}
[North-wind & so]{.strike} West wind\
[A lady says /]{.stage} [poor wife]{.strike} — You'll regret if\
[→ **Fine wife**]{.arrow}
:::
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
When the south wind soft do blow

Then there's not enough wind to go. [Bu\[t\]…]{.side-cue}
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
And me poor wife says to me

We shall starve if you don't go. [So…]{.side-cue}
:::

::: {.annotation}
[(Are you mad – knew wouldn't get out of it)]{.stage} — spoken aside
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
[North Wind]{.cut}\
[Royal Lady (not wife)]{.cut .strike}\
[Poor wife]{.strike} – You will pay
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
Now all you fishermen bold

If you live till you grow old. [Do…]{.side-cue}
:::


---


## Song 2 — The Two Sisters (Oh the Wind & Rain) {.song}

*TRAD. — Peggy Seeger / Bay Whitaker / Fates*

::: {.structure-label}
Intro – our version
:::

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
[A]{.chord} [B]{.chord} [D]{.chord}\
Twas early one morning in the month of May

[Em]{.chord} [A]{.chord}\
*Oh the wind and rain;*

[A]{.chord} [D]{.chord}\
Two sisters went fishing on a hot summer's day,

[Em]{.chord} [D]{.chord} [A]{.chord}\
*[CRYING]{.stage} Oh, the dreadful wind and rain.*
:::
:::

::: {.annotation}
[A]{.circled} — section repeat label
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
[A]{.chord} [D]{.chord} [Em – A]{.chord}\
Two sweet sisters side by side / *Oh…*

[A]{.chord} [D]{.chord} [Em – D – A]{.chord}\
Both of them want to be Johnny's bride / *Oh… [CRYING]{.stage}*
:::

::: {.alt}
a [~~Good~~]{.strike} [Good]{.ins} man's\
or: the same man's
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
[Johnny tells older sister it's not her]{.cut}

→ Johnny gave the young one a fine gold ring / *Oh… /*

::: {.annotation}
He then…
:::

Didn't give the other one anything / *Oh…*
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
Two sweet sisters walking by the stream / *Oh… /*

One came behind & pushed the other one in / *Oh…*
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
She pushed her in to the river to drown / *Oh… /*

And watched her as she floated down / *Oh…*
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
She floated on down to the miller's pond. / *Oh… /*

"Father, father, there comes a swan!" / *Oh…*

::: {.annotation}
[straight to →]{.arrow} "But it wasn't a swan" — then straight into next verse
:::
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
The miller ran for his drifting hook / *Oh… /*

And brought that poor girl from the brook / *Oh…*
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
[He laid her on the bank so dry / *Oh… /*]{.cut}

[A fiddler man came walking by / *Oh…*]{.cut}
:::

::: {.annotation}
both lines marked ✕ (possibly cut)
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
He [saw that poor girl lying]{.strike} [found me / buried]{.ins} there / *Oh… /*

::: {.annotation}
alt: "found me" / "buried" written above "saw that" / "lying"
:::

[He took thirty strands of her long yellow hair / *Oh…*]{.cut}

::: {.annotation}
Fashioned from… And that's exactly what he did
:::
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
He made a fiddle bow of her long yellow hair / *Oh… /*

He made fiddle pegs of her little finger bones / *Oh…*
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
::: {.bracket-group}
He made a fiddle of her little breast-bone / *Oh… /*

With a sound that could melt a heart of stone / *Oh…*

::: {.annotation}
He used to play other fiddles — it would only play…
:::
:::
:::

---

::: {.verse}
And the only tune that fiddle would play / *Oh…*

The only tune that fiddle would play, was / *Oh….*

::: {.annotation}
song they'd sung together – older sister's face
:::
:::

*Oh, the wind and rain.*

*The only tune that the fiddle would play, was oh the dreadful wind and rain*

---


## Song 3 — MacPherson's Lament {.song}

*Bk6 No.10*

::: {.structure-label}
Cue Sheet / Arrangement Notes
:::

| Beat / Story Point | Tune Cue | Ref |
|---|---|---|
| Opening: Poem, This is his tale / tune | [3 McP's]{.hl-green} | [p1]{.circled} |
| Boy being brought up — gypsy + accomplished musician / fiddler | [8 Flowers ~~(crossed out)~~]{.hl-pink .strike} | |
| Father dies – kicked out | MacPh's | [p3]{.circled} |
| Plays for band of gypsies | [18 My Love]{.hl-pink} | |
| Capture of Jannie | [18 part 2]{.hl-blue} | |
| Escape | [Dashing W.S.]{.hl-blue} | |
| No escape | — | |
| Last request | [McPs L]{.hl-yellow} | [3]{.circled} [P5]{.circled} |
| Known as M's Lament = Play in key of… | — | |
| Outro → *(time: 1:50)* | [G with double stopping]{.hl-orange} | |

::: {.annotation}
Paul Anderson – Spotify
:::

::: {.side-box}
**[8]{.circled} Flowers of Edinburgh** — Last 2 lines

**[18]{.circled}** *(My Love She's But A Lassie Yet)*\
[→]{.arrow} bar 1–34\
[→]{.arrow} bar 99–end\
Dashing White Sergeant\
*(partially cut off in source)*
:::


---


## Song 4 — Raggle Taggle {.song}

::: {.corner-note}
Corner note: "muttering = +music"
:::

::: {.structure-label}
Cue Sheet / Arrangement Notes
:::

| Beat / Story Point | Tune Cue |
|---|---|
| Intro: Play [~~together~~]{.strike} | [RTG.]{.hl-yellow} |
| Sense happiness | [Coconut dance (a)]{.hl-orange} |
| Leaving her for a year | [Hidden Valley]{.hl-pink} |
| ↳ Letter – killed | |
| ↳ Duties – bore chn. | [Heidi's Waltz]{.hl-blue} |
| Vagabonds passing | Tam Lin A |
| Gypsies — musicians | Tam Lin B |
| It was her Johnny fair / Heard he'd been married | [Heidi's Waltz **highbit**]{.hl-blue} 657 [D]{.circled} |
| Plan together | |
| Happily ever after | [RTG x2 @ end]{.hl-yellow} ↙ |

::: {.structure-label}
BUT
:::

| Beat / Story Point | Tune Cue |
|---|---|
| Punished JF / Hanged \[by\] | |
| Countess's room | [Hidden Valley]{.hl-pink} |
| Stairs – isolation | [~~Haylands~~]{.strike} [Coconut dance (b)]{.hl-orange} |

[RTG]{.hl-yellow} **to end.**
